reasoning: The Bloomberg Billionaires Index officially updates daily at the close of trading. While Larry Ellison briefly overtook Elon Musk intraday on September 10, 2025, Musk reclaimed the top spot by the end of the day, finishing at $384B over Ellison's $383B. The fact that the Polymarket price was 0.09 in late October implies the market expects this intraday event will not trigger a YES resolution (likely because Bloomberg's official ranking relies on end-of-day print). Furthermore, as of mid-October 2025, Musk's net worth has soared to approximately $453 billion, significantly ahead of Ellison at $367 billion. Given this massive $86 billion cushion and only ~1.5 months left in 2025, the probability of Musk naturally dropping to #2 is exceptionally low. The 6% estimate accommodates slight tail risk of a massive TSLA crash, or a controversial UMA dispute regarding the September intraday overtaking.
